title: Operation Yashimas Mount Futago
wikitext:
[]: Operation Yashima's Mount Futago             Quote Post by TB3 » Sun Oct 28, 2012 3:10 pm Perhaps someone can help with this As well all know, as part of Operation Yashima against the fifth Angel, all of Japan's power was relocated to a sniping position identified as Mount Futago, or Futagoyama. According to the Hakone Eva Map, Futagoyama refers to a pair of twin peaks about 7.5 kilometers south-south-east of Tokyo-3, the highest of which is about 3600 feet datum. However, when you draw a straight line between the two points (on the map or on Google Earth) to depict the course of the particle beam fired by Shinji in Eva-01, you'll notice a problem or two: A: The line goes nowhere near Lake Ashi (despite both depictions in NGE and RoE depicting the beam flying over the lake's surface) B: There's a higher mountain (Kami-yama, approx 4600ft) in the way! Simple artistic error, or did Second Impact or excavating the Geofront cause an entire mountain to collapse? It just seems a bit weird given how particular GAINAX/KHARA are about accuracy when placing the world of Eva in the Hakone region.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Oct 28, 2012 3:59 pm I looked at a map as well as that part in the movie, and I think I see how it could have worked. Ramiel isn't over Lake Ashi when Unit-01 snipes at it: it is over Tokyo-3 which is on the northern shore of the lake. Shinji doesn't aim in a line between the two peaks of Mount Futago: he's sitting on the northwestern peak and aiming towards Tokyo-3. The particle beam passes over the southwestern face of Mount Kami. I'm assuming that same Mount Kami is the mountain that Ramiel partially vaporizes with his counterattack.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Oct 28, 2012 4:12 pm So that people can see what's being discussed, here's the raw map (with the marker provided by Google when searching for Futagoyama, Hakone, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan) ; I must say that the peak labelled Mt Byobu (you may need to zoom out a couple of notches to see it) looks to have a better line of site than where the search indicates.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Oct 28, 2012 4:19 pm Mr. Tines wrote: I must say that the peak labelled Mt Byobu looks to have a better line of site than where the search indicates.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Oct 28, 2012 4:22 pm riffraff11235 wrote: Yeah, I noticed that, too. Maybe they didn't want to be in Ramiel's direct line of sight while setting up the sniping position...?

[]:             Quote Post by TB3 » Sun Oct 28, 2012 5:04 pm riffraff11235 wrote: I looked at a map as well as that part in the movie, and I think I see how it could have worked. Ramiel isn't over Lake Ashi when Unit-01 snipes at it: it is over Tokyo-3 which is on the northern shore of the lake. Shinji doesn't aim in a line between the two peaks of Mount Futago: he's sitting on the northwestern peak and aiming towards Tokyo-3. The particle beam passes over the southwestern face of Mount Kami. I'm assuming that same Mount Kami is the mountain that Ramiel partially vaporizes with his counterattack.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Oct 28, 2012 6:34 pm I think Ramiel's position was a bit farther south than where you placed Tokyo-3 in your first image, TB3. Also, unless I'm getting my perspective completely wrong, that gouged-out mountain we see in the 2.0 shot isn't in the same place as the sniping position that we see in the 1.0 shot. Although I don't see what else it could be....
